L1.area: Customer service messaging and file attachment handling in the back-office administration interface

L2.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in the customer service messaging feature. When a specific feature flag is enabled, a malicious file uploaded through the front-office contact form is served directly to administrators without proper content-type validation or sanitization. When an admin views the attached file in the back office, any embedded scripts execute in the administrator's browser context, potentially allowing session hijacking and unauthorized authenticated actions.

L3.area: Customer service thread message attachment rendering and file serving in the admin panel's customer thread view
L3.description: A cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability exists in the customer service thread messaging feature where file attachments uploaded through the front-office contact form are referenced via a direct publicly accessible URL and served to administrators without content-type restrictions or sanitization. When the customer thread feature flag is enabled, an attacker can upload a crafted file containing malicious JavaScript that executes when an administrator opens the attachment from the back-office customer service thread view, granting access to the admin's session and security tokens.

ground_truth.reason: User-uploaded file attachments from the front-office contact form are served directly via a public URL without content-type validation, allowing an attacker to inject and execute malicious scripts in the administrator's browser when viewing attachments in the back-office customer service thread.
